commands.notes: Documentation (document/framework/testing.md) lists test/test:watch/test:coverage/test:unit/test:verbose scripts using jest, but these are NOT present in the actual package.json. No test command is currently runnable.

ci.notes: No .github/workflows/ directory or CI workflow files detected. document/framework/custom-enviroment.md references a hypothetical .github/workflows/deploy.yml but it does not exist.

dependencies.summary: Runtime: vue, pinia, vue-router, chart.js, @headlessui/vue, @heroicons/vue, heroicons, @tailwindcss/vite, ts-node, uuid. Dev: @types/chart.js, @types/node, @vitejs/plugin-vue, tailwindcss, vite. No test libraries (jest, vitest, @vue/test-utils) are installed despite documentation references.

signals: Single router file: src/router/index.ts with layout-based routing
Pinia stores per module + legacy src/stores/todoStore.js
Entry point: src/main.ts (Vue 3 + Pinia + Vue Router)
conventions: Types: camelCase with .types.ts suffix or index.ts (per module)
Pages: PascalCase with View.vue suffix (preferred) or Page.vue (legacy)

risks_gaps.description: No CI/CD pipeline — no .github/workflows/ exist. Builds, tests, and deployments are entirely manual.

No test runner configured. package.json has no test scripts and no test dependencies installed (jest, vitest, @vue/test-utils). Documentation in document/framework/testing.md describes jest setup but it is aspirational only.
No linting or formatting tools configured. No ESLint config, no Prettier config, no lint/format scripts in package.json.
ts-node@10.9.2 is listed as a runtime dependency rather than a devDependency.
Page naming inconsistency: TodayPage.vue and TodayView.vue both exist in src/pages/. Convention is unclear (View vs Page suffix).
Legacy store migration incomplete: src/stores/todoStore.js (plain JS) still exists alongside typed module-based Pinia stores. Also src/modules/tasks/stores/todoStore.ts exists as the newer version.
ActivityListView.vue is reused for 6 different routes (upcoming, activities, categories, tags, completed, settings) with no route-specific behavior, creating implicit coupling.
heroicons@2.2.0 is listed as a direct dependency alongside @heroicons/vue@2.2.0 — the base heroicons package is likely unnecessary for a Vue project.
Chart.js is globally registered with all registerables in main.ts. This prevents tree-shaking of unused chart types.
